message:
docs(readme): supply periods arg to df.pct_change() (br-derlf / fd90.123)

fd90.123: line 1050 had:

  let pct   = df.pct_change()?;  // Percentage change

But crates/fp-frame/src/lib.rs:25118 declares:
  pub fn pct_change(&self, periods: usize) -> Result<Self, FrameError>

DataFrame::pct_change requires periods (the n-period lag). User would
hit E0061: takes 1 argument but 0 arguments were supplied. The Series
variant at line 5435 also takes periods, so no overload rescues this.

Fix: pass 1 (the pandas default lag) so the example actually compiles.
Inline note documents the contract.

Pure README change. No code impact.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1047,7 +1047,7 @@ let cmax  = df.cummax()?;      // Running maximum
 let cmin  = df.cummin()?;      // Running minimum
 let delta = df.diff(1)?;       // First difference (n periods)
 let moved = df.shift(1)?;      // Shift values by n periods
-let pct   = df.pct_change()?;  // Percentage change
+let pct   = df.pct_change(1)?; // Percentage change (1-period lag, pandas default)
 ```
 
 ### Clipping and Rounding
